hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5/ECL2 complementarity, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding without compromising SSTR2 affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs achieve strong SSTR2 binding but fail to overcome the conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5. Targeted mutagenesis at non-pharmacophore positions is necessary to break off-target contacts and increase selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt conserved pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 that are likely driving off-target binding while preserving pharmacophore positions (7-10) and Cys3/Cys14.

These mutations are expected to break conserved hydrogen bonds and electrostatic interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 binding pockets without compromising the native pharmacophore or disulfide bridge integrity.
